question.text: During operational due diligence of an AIF, which of the following is LEAST relevant?

answer.text: Quality of the fund's back-office operations and compliance systems
IT infrastructure and disaster recovery plans
Adequacy of internal controls and segregation of duties
The personal hobbies of the fund manager's family members

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Operational DD focuses on back-office quality, compliance systems, IT infrastructure, disaster recovery, and internal controls. Personal hobbies of family members have no relevance. **The Trap:** Candidates sometimes confuse 'key person' assessment (which is relevant) with personal life details (which are not). **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series XV (Research Analyst) covers operational risk assessment in a similar framework. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** A robust operational setup reduces the risk of fraud and operational failures — always insist on reviewing the fund's compliance manual.
